column4: grid_cols
question_text
Which statement about work is NOT correct? (Box pushed on frictionless floor)
column9: Work done because net force is not zero

column4: A book sits 2 m above the floor. What form of energy does it primarily have?
Ball A thrown upward at 2x Ball B speed. Ball A kinetic energy is _____ Ball B's.
Roller coaster from rest at top — ignoring friction, what is true at the bottom?
Machine A lifts crate in 10 s; Machine B same height in 2 s. Which is true?
column9: Same work; Machine B has greater power

column4: Pendulum swings with no air resistance. Total mechanical energy _____ throughout.
A student pushes a wall for an hour. How much PHYSICS work is done?
column7: A lot — longer push means more work

column4: A rightward force acts on an object moving LEFT. The force does _____ work.
Why does a thrown ball stop rising and come back down? (Energy conservation)
column7: Air resistance removes all KE at the top
column9: Gravity converts KE to PE going up; PE to KE going down
column11: The ball runs out of force at the top
column13: The ball's mass increases as it rises

column4: A hydroelectric dam energy transformation sequence is:
column7: Electrical to Kinetic to Potential to Thermal
column9: Chemical to Kinetic to Electrical to Thermal
column11: Gravitational PE to KE to Mechanical to Electrical
column13: Nuclear to Thermal to KE to Electrical

column4: Skateboarder converts PE to KE down a half-pipe. She will rise to ___ height on the other side.
column11: No height — KE was used up at bottom

column4: Energy bar chart: ball released from rest at highest point A. Which point has maximum KE?
column9: Point B — lowest height; all PE to KE

column4: At the HIGHEST point of a projectile's trajectory — which is true?
column7: Both horizontal and vertical velocity are zero
column9: Vertical velocity is zero; horizontal is unchanged
column11: Horizontal is zero; vertical is unchanged

column4: A soccer ball kicked at an angle. Which velocity component stays CONSTANT throughout flight?
Ball thrown horizontally from table. Increasing launch HEIGHT will _____ horizontal range.
column9: Increase — more time in the air

column4: Launched at 30° vs 60° with same speed — which travels to a GREATER HEIGHT?
Ball A launched horizontally from cliff; Ball B dropped from same height at same instant. Which lands first?
column7: Ball B — no horizontal motion to slow fall
column11: They land at the same time
column13: Ball A — horizontal speed adds to downward acceleration

column4: Astronaut Dave throws a ball horizontally on the Moon. Compared to Earth, the Moon ball ___.
column9: Falls slower; weaker gravity gives greater horizontal range

column4: Which change would NOT help maximize a stunt car's range off a ramp?
column11: Painting the car a different color
column13: Reducing mass to lower air resistance

column4: At which point along a projectile's path is the total SPEED the smallest?
column11: At the highest point of the trajectory

column4: Fired bullet (horizontal) vs. dropped bullet from same height — which hits ground first?
column9: They land at the same time
column11: Fired — must travel farther; needs more time

column4: Rescue plane at constant altitude drops package. Pilot should release it _____ the hikers.
column9: Before reaching — package arcs forward to land on them

column4: Planet X has 2x Earth's gravity. A 45° projectile on Planet X has ___.
column7: Greater range and height — stronger gravity
column9: Same range; shorter time of flight
column11: Shorter range; lower height; shorter flight time

column4: At Point B (peak of trajectory), the horizontal velocity vector ___.
column9: Points forward; vertical velocity is zero
column11: Is zero; vertical velocity points downward

column4: Which object has the GREATEST momentum?
A net force acts on an object for a period of time. What does this produce?
60 kg skater at rest pushes off wall and moves at 3 m/s. What is the wall's impulse on the skater?
Crumple zones reduce injury in a collision by ___.
column9: Increasing contact time; reducing average force
column11: Decreasing the car's change in momentum
column13: Increasing the car's momentum before impact

column4: Skater A (2x mass) and Skater B push off from rest. Compared to B, Skater A moves at ___.
column7: Twice the speed in same direction
column13: Stays still while B moves away

column4: In a PERFECTLY INELASTIC collision (objects stick together), what is conserved?
Large truck and small car collide head-on and stop. Which force statement is correct?
column7: Truck exerts greater force on car
column9: Car exerts greater force — truck is larger
column11: Both exert equal and opposite forces (Newton's 3rd)
column13: Forces equal only if same speed

column4: Catching a baseball with PADDED mitt hurts less than rigid glove because ___.
column7: Padded mitt reduces total impulse on hand
column11: Padded mitt increases contact time; reducing avg force

column4: Object A: p = 30 kg m/s before and 20 kg m/s after collision. What is A's momentum CHANGE?
Equal-mass carts: A's team pushes B. What happens?
column7: A stays still; B moves right
column9: A moves back slightly; B moves right with more momentum
column11: A and B move away at the same speed

column4: What does a wave transfer from one place to another?
column9: Energy and information without transferring matter

column4: In a TRANSVERSE wave, particles oscillate _____ to the direction of wave travel.
A wave has frequency 5 Hz and wavelength 2 m. What is the wave's speed?
You double frequency while keeping wave speed constant. What happens to wavelength?
Two waves: crest +4 cm meets trough -4 cm at same point. The displacement is ___.
Sound travels faster in steel than air because steel molecules are ___.
column13: Part of transverse waves (which are faster)
